question: [File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted timestamp range query to /api/v1/transactions that causes getTransactionTimestamps() to execute an unbounded full-table scan, delaying all subsequent transaction visibility by stalling the DB connection pool beyond 500% of normal block processing time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply account_id and credit/debit filters simultaneously to force a FULL OUTER JOIN across crypto_transfer, token_transfer, and entity_transaction tables, causing the query to consume enough DB resources to delay transaction ingestion visibility by 500% or more of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTransactionTimestamps()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the open-range timestamp path (eqValues.length === 0) in getTransactionTimestamps() to trigger bindTimestampRange() with a null range, causing the system to query from the very first transaction timestamp to now, effectively freezing transaction query responses for the duration of the scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: bindTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated requests with no timestamp parameter to /api/v1/transactions when bindTimestampRange is enabled, causing getFirstTransactionTimestamp() to anchor the range at genesis and force maxTransactionsTimestampRangeNs-wide scans on every request, cumulatively delaying block-level transaction visibility by 500% or more?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: doGetTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user flood /api/v1/transactions with concurrent requests that each trigger cache misses in cache.get(), forcing parallel getTransactionsDetails() DB queries that collectively saturate the connection pool and delay new block transaction visibility by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTransactionsByIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a valid transaction hash to /api/v1/transactions/:transactionIdOrHash that triggers the mayMissLongTermScheduledTransaction() retry path, causing two sequential pool.queryQuietly() calls with maxScheduledTransactionConsensusTimestampRangeNs-wide ranges, doubling DB load and delaying transaction confirmation visibility beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/transactions.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getTransactionsByTransactionIdsSql()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction ID whose valid_start_ns is near the minimum BigInt value, causing minTimestamp - maxValidStartTimestampDriftNs to underflow and produce a consensus_timestamp >= condition that scans the entire transaction table, freezing query throughput for the duration of the scan?
